issue: haus.nestjs-graphql-patterns is selected with score=70 (repo-role-match + stack-match) and surfaced at medium confidence even though the repo has no @nestjs/* dependency and no NestJS source. The rule is intended for NestJS GraphQL APIs; here it is a false positive that will load NestJS-specific guidance into a pure codegen project.
expected: Rules whose id/title signals NestJS should require an actual NestJS signal (dep `@nestjs/core` or role `nestjs-api`), not satisfy on `graphql-api` role alone.
rootCauseHypothesis: haus.nestjs-graphql-patterns has `repoRoles: ["nestjs-api", "graphql-api"]` (manifest entry covers both). graphql-api role alone is enough to trigger repo-role-match (+40) and the graphql tag triggers stack-match (+30), clearing the 40-point cutoff with no NestJS signal required.
